prerequisites: An AWS account with Amazon DynamoDB in the target region.
An IAM identity (IAM user) with read and write permissions on the DynamoDB tables the connector will write to.
An AWS access key pair (access key ID and secret access key) for that IAM identity.
The AWS region where your DynamoDB tables reside (dynamodb_region).
A table name prefix (dynamodb_table_name_prefix); the connector names tables as prefix_streamname.
Network connectivity from the connector to the DynamoDB endpoint.

authMethods.name: AWS access key (access_key_id / secret_access_key)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a long-term AWS access key pair. Provide the IAM user's access key ID in access_key_id and its secret access key in secret_access_key. Both values are used together to sign requests to DynamoDB, and the IAM identity must have read and write permissions on the target tables.

setupSteps.title: Create an IAM user with DynamoDB write access
setupSteps.body: In the AWS IAM console, create (or choose) a dedicated IAM user for the connector and grant it read and write permissions on the DynamoDB tables it will use. AWS recommends not using account root credentials.

setupSteps.title: Generate an access key pair
setupSteps.body: For that IAM user, create an access key. AWS returns an access key ID (for example AKIA...) and a secret access key. Save the secret access key immediately, since it can be retrieved only at creation time; if lost you must delete the key and create a new one.

setupSteps.title: Set the access credentials
setupSteps.body: Enter the access key ID in the access_key_id field and the secret access key in the secret_access_key field. Both are required and stored as secrets.

setupSteps.title: Configure region and table prefix
setupSteps.body: Set dynamodb_region to the AWS region of your DynamoDB tables and set dynamodb_table_name_prefix to the prefix used for created tables (the connector names tables as prefix_streamname). Both fields are required.

setupSteps.title: Optionally set a custom endpoint
setupSteps.body: Leave dynamodb_endpoint empty to use standard AWS DynamoDB. Set it to a custom URL only when targeting an alternative or local DynamoDB endpoint. This field is optional.

sources.title: AWS Documentation - Manage access keys for IAM users
